description:
Advances: none (test infrastructure only — no shipped-code behavior change; kind: command and tagged_tests verification paths in lib are untouched)

Deliverable:
Stage 1 of the async unlock specced in session bbf3b419 (2026-07-02). The suite currently runs 163.7s sync / 10.8s async (measured post-hermetic-git, 816 tests) because SpecLedEx.Case hardcodes async: false for all 33 files that use it. The only global mutation justifying that is Mix.shell(Mix.Shell.Process) in its setup — but the value is a constant across all tests and Mix.Shell.Process routes messages to the calling process, so the mechanism is per-process-safe once the global write moves out of per-test setup. Tests invoke task modules' run/1 directly (zero Mix.Task.run calls in test code), so per-test Mix.Task.reenable is vestigial.

Changes:
- test/test_helper.exs: set Mix.shell(Mix.Shell.Process) once, before ExUnit.start().
- test_support/specled_ex_case.ex: remove Mix.shell/1 and reenable_tasks() from setup (keep Mix.Shell.Process.flush() — it is per-process). Accept an async option in using/1 (use SpecLedEx.Case, async: false) with async: true as the default.
- Remove the dead per-file Mix.Task.reenable calls (spec_check_test.exs:113, spec_review_task_test.exs:5, spec_suggest_binding_test.exs:6, spec_dedup_realized_by_test.exs:12).
- Flip eligible files to async (default), keeping an explicit async: false opt-out on the serial rump:
  * realization/binding_test.exs (File.cd! — VM-global cwd)
  * verifier_test.exs (System.put_env PATH shim) and spec_check_test.exs (SPECLED_SHOW_INFO env)
  * compiler/xref_test.exs + spec_triangle_test.exs if they reach Compiler.Xref.load/1 (Mix.Task.run("xref") — global task state)
  * compiler/tracer_test.exs (named ETS table)
  * IntegrationCase users + spec_cover_test_test.exs (shared test/fixtures/*/_build, concurrent child mix compile clashes)
  * realization files with same-name fixture modules recompiled/purged across tests in one file (typespecs_test.exs, expanded_behavior_test.exs, use_test.exs, implication_integration_test.exs, orchestrator_test.exs — audit each; async runs tests within a file concurrently)
- Flip in batches with a full suite run between batches; latent inter-test coupling hidden by serial execution is the main risk.

Explicitly NOT in scope (Stage 2 follow-up candidates, do not do here): injectable runner seam for Compiler.Xref.load/1; removing File.cd! from binding_test; parameterizing verifier command lookup to avoid PATH mutation; per-file failure attribution for the merged tagged_tests run.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: full suite (mix test --include integration), 0 failures, run at least twice consecutively to catch async flakiness.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: ExUnit summary shows the bulk of runtime moved from sync to async; target total wall time ~60-90s on the reference host (from ~174s post-hermetic-git baseline). Record before/after times in the closing comment.
- Commands: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test && mix format --check-formatted && mix deps.unlock --check-unused && mix spec.check

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- test/test_helper.exs
- test_support/specled_ex_case.ex
- test/**/*.exs (async flags + dead reenable removal only — no assertion or logic changes beyond what an async flip strictly requires)
- .spec/state.json (regenerated only)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/**
- test/fixtures/**
- .spec/specs/**
- .spec/config.yml

Out of Scope (intent):
- No changes to shipped verification behavior — kind: command per-command execution and the merged tagged_tests run are untouched.
- Do not weaken or delete tests to make them async-compatible; a test that cannot be made async without logic changes stays async: false with a one-line comment naming its blocker.
- Do not revisit verification.command_timeout_ms here.

Dependencies: depends on specled_-s65.4 (hermetic git config) — both touch test_helper.exs and the async win is only measurable on top of it.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] mix test passes (full suite, --include integration, two consecutive green runs)
- [ ] mix spec.check green
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only test/, test_support/ (+ regenerated .spec/state.json)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Files touched: test/test_helper.exs, test_support/specled_ex_case.ex, test/integration/mixed_debug_info_fixture_test.exs, test/integration/scenario_macro_provider_drift_test.exs, test/integration/scenario_refactor_stable_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_review_task_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_suggest_binding_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_triangle_test.exs, test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_integration_test.exs, test/specled_ex/compiler/tracer_test.exs, test/specled_ex/compiler/xref_test.exs, test/specled_ex/mix/tasks/spec_dedup_realized_by_test.exs, test/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary_test.exs, test/specled_ex/realization/binding_test.exs, test/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior_test.exs, test/specled_ex/realization/hash_store_test.exs, test/specled_ex/realization/hash_store_versioning_test.exs, test/specled_ex/realization/implementation_test.exs, test/specled_ex/realization/implication_integration_test.exs, test/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator_test.exs, test/specled_ex/realization/typespecs_test.exs, test/specled_ex/realization/use_test.exs, test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs
Tests added: none — async-flag-only change
Verification stubs flipped: none

Summary: SpecLedEx.Case now sets Mix.Shell.Process once from test_helper, defaults to async: true, keeps per-process shell flushing, and no longer reenables Mix tasks from every setup; the dead per-file reenable calls named in the ticket were removed. Explicit async: false opt-outs remain for VM-global cwd/env/PATH/Mix xref/ETS/shared tracer-manifest/shared fixture-build/module-recompile/compiler-option blockers, while isolated direct realization store/refactor tests moved to async. Baseline was real 58.75s with ExUnit 44.2s (1.2s async, 42.9s sync); final consecutive runs were real 24.04s and 21.44s, with the last ExUnit summary 20.6s (13.8s async, 6.8s sync).

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6
Log: /Users/mz/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 25 (test/test_helper.exs, test_support/specled_ex_case.ex, + 23 test/**/*.exs)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0 (all under test/**, plus the explicitly-allowed test_support/specled_ex_case.ex and test/test_helper.exs)
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (no lib/**, no test/fixtures/**, no .spec/specs/**, no .spec/config.yml; .spec/state.json not committed, regenerated locally by spec.check as expected)
- Non-async-flag changes in test files: 0 beyond scope. The only non-flag edits are (a) removal of the 4 dead Mix.Task.reenable calls named in the ticket - spec_check_test.exs:113, spec_review_task_test.exs setup, spec_suggest_binding_test.exs setup, spec_dedup_realized_by_test.exs setup - and (b) one-line rationale comments above async: false lines. No assertion or test-logic changes.

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 0 (Advances: none - no spec subject), passed: N/A
- Core-claim audit (read test_support/specled_ex_case.ex + test/test_helper.exs directly): PASS.
  * Mix.shell(Mix.Shell.Process) moved to test_helper.exs, called once before ExUnit.start(). CONFIRMED.
  * SpecLedEx.Case setup no longer calls Mix.shell/1 or reenable_tasks(). CONFIRMED.
  * Mix.Shell.Process.flush() still called per-test in setup. CONFIRMED (per-process safe).
  * `using opts` threads async: `use ExUnit.Case, async: Keyword.get(unquote(opts), :async, true)`; default true, and `use SpecLedEx.Case, async: false` correctly resolves to `use ExUnit.Case, async: false`. Verified via the macro, not just the summary. IntegrationCase does NOT override async in its `using` block, so its CaseTemplate proxy defaults to `use ExUnit.Case` = async: false (safe default); the two in-scope IntegrationCase users set async: false explicitly anyway.
- Per-file async-flag audit: 25 files reviewed; all correct; 0 flagged WRONG.
  * ~16 async: false files each have a real blocking pattern (verified by grep + reads): File.cd! (binding_test); System.put_env PATH (verifier_test); SPECLED_SHOW_INFO env (spec_check_test); Mix.Task.run("xref") via Xref.load (xref_test); named ETS + Code.put_compiler_option(:tracers) (tracer_test); Code.put_compiler_option(:debug_info) (scenario_macro_provider_drift, typespecs, expanded_behavior, implication_integration, use); per-test same-name Kernel.ParallelCompiler recompile + :code.purge/:code.delete (typespecs, expanded_behavior, use, implication_integration, orchestrator, implementation, binding); IntegrationCase shared test/fixtures _build (mixed_debug_info, spec_cover_test, manifest_integration); Tracer shared _build manifest (spec_triangle).
  * Files flipped/defaulted to async: true - spot-checked all module-compiling ones and the pure-logic ones: api_boundary_test (compiles fixtures ONCE in setup_all, globally-unique module names ApiBoundaryFixtures.*, per-test isolated root) SAFE; scenario_refactor_stable (single test, unique names ScenarioFixture.A/B unused elsewhere, no debug_info/env/cwd/ETS mutation) SAFE; scenario_tier_dispatch (compiles once in setup_all, unique name TierDispatchFixture.Mod, read-only in tests) SAFE; hash_store_test + hash_store_versioning_test (isolated per-test tmp dirs) SAFE. Comprehensive grep over the ENTIRE test tree found every File.cd/System.put_env/Application.put_env/named-ETS/Code.put_compiler_option/Mix.Task.run occurrence lives ONLY in async: false files.
  * reenable_tasks/0 still called in spec_next_task_test, spec_tasks_test, spec_status_task_test (now async: true by default): harmless. Zero tests call Mix.Task.run; the only two lib Mix.Task.run sites (Xref.load -> "xref", spec.cover.test -> "test") are reached only by async: false tests (xref_test, tracer_test, spec_cover_test_test). So the leftover reenable calls are vestigial global-state writes nothing reads.
  * File.cwd!() reads in branch_check_test:421 (git show HEAD:.spec/state.json) and tagged_tests_test:154 (subprocess cd:) are read-only; the sole File.cd! mutator (binding_test) is async: false, so during the async phase cwd is stable at project root. SAFE.
  * ExUnit scheduling barrier confirmed by reading runner.ex (Elixir 1.18.1) lines 115-130: sync (async: false) modules run only AFTER all async modules complete, each running alone. So every VM-global mutator placed in async: false gets exclusive VM access - the whole design is sound.
- Requirements coverage: N/A (Advances: none). Fix-correctness assessment: PASS - every async flag is defensible; async: false rump matches the ticket's named blockers with real code evidence; async: true set contains no VM-global mutation.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. lib/** is untouched, so shipped verification behavior - kind: command per-command execution and the merged tagged_tests run - is unchanged.
- Project verification: PASS. Each mix test --include integration run (fresh random seed each time -> different concurrent interleaving):
  * Run 1: seed 836898 - 2 properties, 820 tests, 0 failures. Finished 23.3s (12.4s async / 10.9s sync); wall real 24.47s. PASS
  * Run 2: seed 222744 - 2 properties, 820 tests, 0 failures. Finished 21.6s (11.5s async / 10.1s sync); wall real 22.59s. PASS
  * Run 3: seed 286103 - 2 properties, 820 tests, 0 failures. Finished 22.3s (14.1s async / 8.2s sync); wall real 23.31s. PASS
  * mix compile --warnings-as-errors: PASS (exit 0). mix format --check-formatted: PASS (exit 0). mix deps.unlock --check-unused: PASS (exit 0). mix spec.check: PASS (status=pass, errors=0, warnings=0, exit 0; 22 branch findings are informational branch-guard output from 42 changed files, not verification failures).
- Audit-expanded scope, if any: none (all touched files were within the Allowed globs).

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory only):
- spec_triangle_test.exs carries the rationale comment "Tracer.manifest_path/0 points at a shared _build manifest file" rather than the ticket's tentative "if it reaches Compiler.Xref.load/1" framing. The file aliases Tracer and stays async: false; the conclusion (serial) is correct and safe regardless of which blocker you cite.
- 3 files (spec_next_task_test, spec_tasks_test, spec_status_task_test) still contain vestigial reenable_tasks calls now that they run async. Harmless today (no test uses Mix.Task.run), but a Stage 2 sweep could remove them and drop the now-only-used-there reenable_tasks/0 helper. Not required by this ticket, which named only 4 specific removals.

title: Unlock async ExUnit — drop the Mix.shell serialization from SpecLedEx.Case
